key_topics: Number: reading and ordering numbers to 10,000
Negative numbers: reading and ordering on a number line
Rounding to nearest 10, 100, 1000
Addition and subtraction of numbers up to 10,000
Multiplication: 7, 8, 9 times tables; multiplying TU × U and TU × TU
Division: dividing three-digit numbers by one-digit, including remainders
Fractions: mixed numbers, improper fractions, adding and subtracting like fractions
Equivalence between fractions and decimals (tenths and hundredths)
Ordering decimals with one and two decimal places
Percentages: finding simple percentages (10%, 25%, 50%)
Geometry: properties of triangles and quadrilaterals
Symmetry: reflective and rotational symmetry
Co-ordinates in the first quadrant
Perimeter and area of rectangles and compound shapes
Angles: acute, obtuse, right, reflex — measuring with a protractor
Data: line graphs, frequency tables, mode, median, mean, range
competences: Add and subtract numbers up to 10,000 efficiently
Convert between fractions, decimals, and percentages
Measure and draw angles with a protractor
Find mode, median, mean, and range of data sets
typical_mistakes: Confusing mixed numbers and improper fractions when converting
Measuring angles from wrong baseline on protractor
Adding tenths and hundredths without aligning decimal points

key_topics: Reading: inference and deduction from longer, complex texts
Identifying how authors create characters and settings
Non-fiction: comparing texts on the same topic
Point of view: how text type affects content and language
Writing: narrative with developed characters and settings
Formal and informal writing styles: letters, emails
Explanation texts: cause and effect language
Argument texts: presenting both sides of an issue
Grammar: relative clauses, fronted adverbials
Punctuation: commas after fronted adverbials, inverted commas for dialogue
Vocabulary: synonyms, antonyms, word formation
competences: Read and analyse complex texts, making inferences with evidence
Write narrative texts with well-developed characters and atmosphere
Distinguish formal and informal registers and apply appropriately
Use relative clauses and fronted adverbials in writing
typical_mistakes: Using speech marks incorrectly in dialogue
Not distinguishing formal from informal register

key_topics: Humans and animals: digestive system, teeth and diet
Skeleton and muscles: support, protection, movement
Plants: photosynthesis, pollination, seed dispersal
Electricity: circuits, conductors and insulators, symbols
Electrical components: switches, bulbs, buzzers, motors
Sound: how sound travels, pitch and loudness, vibration
States of matter: evaporation, condensation, the water cycle
Rocks: rock types (igneous, sedimentary, metamorphic), fossils, soil
competences: Describe the human digestive system and its functions
Build and draw simple electrical circuits
Describe the water cycle and explain evaporation and condensation
Classify rocks and explain how fossils form
typical_mistakes: Thinking plants get energy from soil rather than from light
Not drawing the water cycle arrows in the correct direction
Confusing igneous and sedimentary rock formation
